Administrative services
Administrative services make up one of the largest staff groups in the NHSScotland workforce. The NHS relies on people working in administrative roles to make sure healthcare services run efficiently and smoothly every day.

title: Allied health professions | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Allied health professions
You can be an allied health professional
Allied health professionals (AHPs) use their unique skills and knowledge to improve people's lives.
You may already have heard of a physiotherapist, a dietitian, or a paramedic. Let's explore all the AHP job roles in the NHSScotland workforce.
Who are AHPs?
Allied health professionals are the third largest clinical workforce in NHSScotland. AHPs assess, diagnose, and treat people of all ages to enhance their

title: Allied health professions | NHSScotland Careers
content:
ical workforce in NHSScotland. AHPs assess, diagnose, and treat people of all ages to enhance their health, wellbeing, and quality of life. AHPs also signpost people to other services to help them access additional care and support they may need.
Watch our video to learn more about all 14 AHP careers.
Art therapist
Diagnostic radiographer
Dietitian
Dramatherapist
Music therapist
Occupational therapist
Orthoptist
Orthotist
Paramedic
Physiotherapist
Podiatrist
Prosthetist
Speech and language thera

title: Allied health professions | NHSScotland Careers
content:
pist
Orthoptist
Orthotist
Paramedic
Physiotherapist
Podiatrist
Prosthetist
Speech and language therapist
Therapeutic radiographer
AHP practice educator
Innovative and diverse roles
You could be an AHP working in clinical practice in a wide range of environments, including community settings, GP practices, hospitals, patients' homes, prison services, and schools.
AHPs also work in:
evidence, research, and development to inform practice and improve services
roles that facilitate learning to suppor

title: Allied health professions | NHSScotland Careers
content:
ch, and development to inform practice and improve services
roles that facilitate learning to support the education and development of others
leadership roles in leading teams and managing services
There are so many possibilities for AHPs in the NHS.
Help shape the future of AHP education in Scotland
Discover the benefits of becoming an AHP practice educator or contributing to placement delivery.
Become an AHP practice educator

title: Ambulance services | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Ambulance services
Ambulance services
The Scottish Ambulance Service ensures an immediate response with the most appropriate skills and equipment is provided to 999 emergency calls to save lives. This could involve an ambulance, rapid response car, motorcycle, bicycle or air ambulance.
The ambulance transport team drives non-emergency patients to hospitals for appointments or routine admissions, and to their home following discharge from hospital.
Ambulance care assistant

title: Health play services | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Health play services
Health play services
Health play services use therapeutic play to help children and young people, their families, and carers have a positive experience of healthcare. It aids in the understanding of illness and treatment, reduces anxiety, and provides relaxation in unfamiliar surroundings.
What do health play services do?
Health play specialists can guide children through role-play and therapeutic play to help them:
express feelings
interact with medical

title: Health play services | NHSScotland Careers
content:
children through role-play and therapeutic play to help them:
express feelings
interact with medical equipment
relieve boredom
become comfortable in unfamiliar surroundings
build confidence within hospital and clinical staff
Health play specialists need a foundation degree in Healthcare Play Specialism. Health play assistants also work within the play team and have childcare qualifications.
Watch this video produced by NHS Lothian to find out more about what play services do to support and care

title: Health play services | NHSScotland Careers
content:
this video produced by NHS Lothian to find out more about what play services do to support and care for children in hospital.
Health play specialist
Health play assistant

title: Healthcare science | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Healthcare science
Healthcare science
The healthcare science workforce is involved in 80% of diagnostic decisions in patient care. They’re at the heart of improving healthcare and advancing scientific development through technology and innovation.
Who are healthcare scientists?
Did you know that there are more than 50 different healthcare science careers?
Healthcare scientists use science, data, product design, and engineering to:
prevent, diagnose, monitor, and treat medica

title: Healthcare science | NHSScotland Careers
content:
use science, data, product design, and engineering to:
prevent, diagnose, monitor, and treat medical conditions
design and develop custom medical devices
commission and maintain medical equipment
make, improve, and adapt assistive technology
There are many routes to a rewarding healthcare science career, including Modern Apprenticeships, entry-level support roles, and training programmes.
Engineering sciences
Health informatics sciences
Laboratory sciences
Physical and imaging sciences
Physiolo

title: Healthcare science | NHSScotland Careers
content:
ring sciences
Health informatics sciences
Laboratory sciences
Physical and imaging sciences
Physiological sciences
A career for you in healthcare science
Ready to discover some of the healthcare science careers you can choose in the NHS? Check out our new career guide!

Medical associate professions
Medical associate professions
Medical associate professions (MAPs) include physician associates, anaesthesia associates, and surgical care practitioners. They are trained healthcare professionals who provide patient care under supervision. MAPs work in multidisciplinary teams with doctors, nurses, and other healthcare workers.
Anaesthesia associate
Physician associate

title: Medicine | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Medicine
Medicine
Choose a career in medicine where you can make a positive impact on people's lives. With over 65 different specialties, medicine offers a rewarding and purpose-driven career path.
You can be a doctor in the NHS
Did you know that in Scotland, there are 5 medical schools where you can study medicine? After your undergraduate degree, you'll receive world-class postgraduate training in teaching hospitals and community healthcare settings to become a doctor.
If

title: Medicine | NHSScotland Careers
content:
ostgraduate training in teaching hospitals and community healthcare settings to become a doctor.
If you don't have the qualifications for medical school, there are access routes for school leavers and adults returning to education.
Anaesthetist
General practitioner
Ophthalmologist
Surgeon

title: Midwifery | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Midwifery
Midwifery
Midwives and health visitors help prepare new parents for parenthood by supporting them in making lifestyle changes to benefit their future family life. Being a midwife covers much more than delivering babies, although that’s a crucial part of the job.
If you would like to support mothers during pregnancy and ensure the best quality of care for babies at the earliest stages of life, this could be the career for you.
Midwife
Health visitor

title: NHS 24 service delivery | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
NHS 24 service delivery
NHS 24 service delivery
NHS 24 is a patient-focused service that operates 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. It provides advice and assistance to people over the phone and online, helping them get the right care in the right place.
NHS 24 unscheduled care
NHS 24's unscheduled care service was established in 2004. It receives 1.5 million calls every year from people across Scotland on the free 111 phone number.
All calls are evaluated by a triage team an

title: NHS 24 service delivery | NHSScotland Careers
content:
rom people across Scotland on the free 111 phone number.
All calls are evaluated by a triage team and directed to a healthcare professional, where appropriate. In a life or limb-threatening emergency, calls are transferred to the Scottish Ambulance Service.
Discover some of the jobs available at NHS 24.
Call handler
Psychological wellbeing practitioner
Pay and benefits
Find out about our flexible working policies, pay rates, pensions, health and wellbeing initiatives, and career development supp

title: NHS 24 service delivery | NHSScotland Careers
content:
working policies, pay rates, pensions, health and wellbeing initiatives, and career development support.

title: Nursing | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Nursing
Nursing
The key role of a nurse is to deliver person-centred, safe and effective care. Nurses help patients understand information about their diagnoses and treatment. They also respond to each patient’s physical, clinical, and emotional needs.
Areas of nursing practice
In the UK, there are 4 areas of practice. These are:
adult nursing
children’s nursing
learning disabilities nursing
mental health nursing
Whichever route you take into nursing, you’ll study a degree p

title: Nursing | NHSScotland Careers
content:
ilities nursing
mental health nursing
Whichever route you take into nursing, you’ll study a degree programme in your chosen practice area.
Adult nurse
Children's nurse
Learning disability nurse
Mental health nurse
Operating department practitioner
Prison nurse

title: Optometry | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Optometry
Optometry
Working in vision care offers many rewarding career opportunities. As an optometrist, you'll examine a person's eyes to make sure they get the right optical treatment and access to healthcare services. As a dispensing optician, you'll provide advice and supply and fit glasses suitable for a person's lifestyle and vocational needs.
Dispensing optician
Optometrist

title: Pharmacy | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Pharmacy
Pharmacy
Do you enjoy helping and supporting others? A career in pharmacy services could be for you if you like science subjects like Biology and Chemistry. You'll also need good communication skills to work with patients and the public.
Make a difference with a career in pharmacy services
In hospitals, pharmacy staff provide advice and support to patients on how to take their medicines. They identify the medicines patients were taking before hospital admission and

title: Pharmacy | NHSScotland Careers
content:
ake their medicines. They identify the medicines patients were taking before hospital admission and make sure they are still appropriate when the patient returns home.
Pharmacy staff work in primary care too. They see patients who need help with their medicines. They also treat people with long-term conditions, such as asthma and diabetes.
Community pharmacies are where people can buy over-the-counter products for coughs, aches, and pains. They can also get their prescriptions dispensed and rece

title: Pharmacy | NHSScotland Careers
content:
nter products for coughs, aches, and pains. They can also get their prescriptions dispensed and receive lifestyle advice for better health.
Pharmacist
Pharmacy support worker
Pharmacy technician

title: Psychology | NHSScotland Careers
content:
Home
Explore careers
Psychology
Psychology
Psychology is the study of how people think, feel and behave. Through specialist techniques, such as psychometric testing and observation, psychologists investigate the causes of behaviour and the mental processes involved.
Psychologists work closely with patients to manage and treat their condition to improve mental health and wellbeing.
Assistant psychologist
Clinical associate in applied psychology
Clinical psychologist
Counselling psychologist
Foren
